title: Genesis 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 In the beginning, God made the sky and the earth.
2 The earth was empty and shapeless. Darkness covered the deep waters. The Spirit of God was moving over the waters.
3 Then God said, "Let there be light!" And there was light.
4 God saw that the light was good. He kept the light apart from the darkness.
5 God called the light "day." He called the darkness "night." Then evening came, and morning came — the first day.
6 God said, "Let there be a wide space between the waters. It will keep the waters apart."
7 So God made the wide space. He kept the waters below it apart from the waters above. And it was so.
8 God called the wide space "sky." Then evening came, and morning came — the second day.
9 God said, "Let the waters under the sky come together in one place. Let dry land appear." And it was so.
10 God called the dry land "earth." He called the waters that came together "seas." And God saw that it was good.
11 Then God said, "Let the earth bring forth plants with seeds in them. Let there be fruit trees that grow fruit with seeds inside, each of its own kind." And it was so.
12 The earth grew plants with seeds in them, each of its own kind. It grew trees that bear fruit with seeds inside, each of its own kind. And God saw that it was good.
13 Then evening came, and morning came — the third day.
14 God said, "Let there be lights in the sky. Let them keep the day apart from the night. Let them be signs to mark seasons, days, and years.
15 They will shine from the sky and give light on the earth." And it was so.
16 God made two great lights — the bigger light to rule the day, and the smaller light to rule the night. He also made the stars.
17 God put the lights in the sky to give light on the earth.
18 They were to rule the day and the night. They kept the light apart from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.
19 Then evening came, and morning came — the fourth day.
20 God said, "Let the waters be full of living things. Let birds fly above the earth in the open sky."
21 So God made the huge sea animals. He made every living thing that moves in the waters, each of its own kind. He made every kind of bird that flies. And God saw that it was good.
22 Then God blessed them. He said, "Have many children. Fill the waters of the seas. And let the birds grow in number on the earth."
23 Then evening came, and morning came — the fifth day.
24 God said, "Let the earth bring forth living things, each of its own kind — farm animals, small animals that move on the ground, and wild animals, each of its own kind." And it was so.

25 God made the wild animals, each of its own kind. He made the farm animals, each of its own kind. He made every small animal that moves on the ground, each of its own kind. And God saw that it was good.

26 Then God said, "Let us make people in our image, to be like us. They will rule over the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, the farm animals, the wild animals, and every small animal that moves on the earth."
27 So God made people in his own image. In the image of God, he made them. He made them male and female.
28 God blessed them. He said to them, "Have many children. Fill the earth and take care of it. Rule over the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, and every animal that moves on the earth."
29 Then God said, "Look — I have given you every plant on the earth that has seeds. I have given you every tree with fruit that has seeds. They will be your food.

30 I have given every green plant for food to every wild animal, every bird of the sky, and every small animal that moves on the earth — to every creature that has the breath of life in it." And it was so.
31 God looked at everything he had made. And it was very good. Then evening came, and morning came — the sixth day.

title: Genesis 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 So the sky and the earth were finished, with everything in them.
2 By the seventh day, God had finished his work. So on that day he rested from all his work.
3 Then God blessed the seventh day and made it holy. On that day he rested from all the work of creation.
4 This is the story of the sky and the earth when they were made. It was the day the LORD God made them.
5 No bush of the field had yet grown on the earth. No wild plant had yet come up. The LORD God had not sent rain on the earth. And there was no man to work the ground.
6 But water came up from the earth and wet the whole ground.
7 Then the LORD God made the man from the dust of the ground. He breathed the breath of life into the man's nose. And the man became a living person.
8 The LORD God planted a garden in Eden, in the east. He put the man he had made in the garden.
9 The LORD God grew every tree from the ground. They were nice to look at and good for food. In the middle of the garden were the tree of life and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
10 A river flowed out of Eden to water the garden. From there it split into four rivers.
11 The name of the first river is Pishon. It runs through the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold.
12 The gold of that land is pure. Bdellium gum and onyx stone are also found there.
13 The name of the second river is Gihon. It runs through the whole land of Cush.
14 The name of the third river is Hiddekel. It runs along the east side of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
15 Then the LORD God took the man and put him in the Garden of Eden. He told him to work it and care for it.
16 The LORD God gave the man this command: "You may eat freely from every tree of the garden.
17 But you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil. On the day that you eat from it, you will surely die."
18 The LORD God also said, "It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper who fits him."

19 The LORD God made every wild animal and every bird of the sky from the ground. He brought them to the man to see what he would name each one. Whatever the man called each living creature, that was its name.
20 The man gave names to all the farm animals, the birds of the sky, and every wild animal. But for Adam, no helper who fits him was found.
21 So the LORD God made the man fall into a deep sleep. While he slept, God took one of the man's ribs. Then he closed up the place with flesh.
22 From the rib the LORD God had taken from the man, he made a woman. He brought her to the man.
23 And the man said: "This is now part of my bones and part of my body. She will be called 'woman,' because she was taken out of man."
24 For this reason, a man will leave his father and mother and join with his wife. And the two will become one flesh.
25 The man and his wife were both naked. And they felt no shame.

title: Genesis 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 The serpent was more clever than any wild animal the LORD God had made. He said to the woman, "Did God really say, 'You must not eat from any tree in the garden'?"
2 The woman answered the serpent, "We may eat the fruit from the trees in the garden.
3 But about the tree in the middle of the garden, God has said, 'You must not eat from it. You must not even touch it, or you will die.'"
4 "You will not really die," the serpent said to her.
5 "God knows that when you eat from it, your eyes will be opened. You will be like God, knowing good and evil."

6 The woman saw that the tree was good for food. It looked beautiful. It was also good for making her wise. So she took the fruit and ate it. She gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it too.
7 Then the eyes of both of them were opened. They knew that they were naked. So they sewed fig leaves together and made coverings for themselves.
8 Then the man and his wife heard the voice of the LORD God. He was walking in the garden in the cool of the day. They hid from the LORD God among the trees of the garden.
9 But the LORD God called out to the man, "Where are you?"
10 He answered, "I heard Your voice in the garden. I was afraid because I was naked. So I hid myself."
11 The LORD God asked, "Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten from the tree I told you not to eat from?"
12 The man answered, "The woman You gave me — she gave me fruit from the tree, and I ate it."
13 Then the LORD God said to the woman, "What is this you have done?" The woman replied, "The serpent tricked me, and I ate."

14 So the LORD God said to the serpent: "Because you have done this, you are cursed more than all farm animals and all wild animals. You will crawl on your belly. You will eat dust all the days of your life.
15 I will put hostility between you and the woman. I will put hostility between your offspring and her offspring. He will crush your head, and you will strike his heel."
16 To the woman He said: "I will greatly increase your pain when you give birth. In pain you will bring forth children. You will want your husband, but he will rule over you."

17 And to Adam He said: "You listened to the voice of your wife. You ate from the tree I told you not to eat from. So the ground is cursed because of you. Through hard work you will eat from it all the days of your life.
18 It will grow thorns and weeds for you. You will eat the plants of the field.
19 By the sweat of your face you will eat your bread. You will work until you return to the ground. You were taken from the ground. For you are dust, and to dust you will return."
20 Adam named his wife Eve. She would be the mother of all who live.
21 Then the LORD God made clothes from animal skin for Adam and his wife. He clothed them.
22 The LORD God said, "Look — the man has become like one of Us. He now knows good and evil. He must not reach out his hand and take also from the tree of life. He must not eat it and live forever."
23 So the LORD God sent him out of the Garden of Eden. He sent him to work the ground he had been taken from.
24 So He drove the man out. He placed cherubim on the east side of the Garden of Eden. He also placed a flashing sword of fire to guard the way to the tree of life.

title: Genesis 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Adam slept with his wife Eve. She became pregnant and gave birth to Cain. She said, "With the help of the LORD I have brought forth a man."
2 Later she gave birth to Cain's brother Abel. Abel took care of sheep. Cain worked the ground.
3 After some time, Cain brought some fruit of the ground as an offering to the LORD.
4 Abel brought the best parts of the firstborn of his flock. The LORD was pleased with Abel and his offering.
5 But He was not pleased with Cain and his offering. So Cain became very angry, and his face looked down.
6 The LORD said to Cain, "Why are you angry? Why does your face look down?
7 If you do what is right, will you not be accepted? But if you refuse, sin is waiting at your door like a wild animal. It wants to catch you, but you must rule over it."
8 Then Cain said to his brother Abel, "Let's go out to the field." While they were in the field, Cain attacked his brother Abel and killed him.
9 The LORD said to Cain, "Where is your brother Abel?" Cain answered, "I do not know! Am I my brother's keeper?"
10 The LORD replied, "What have you done? Your brother's blood cries out to me from the ground.
11 Now you are cursed and sent away from the ground. The ground has opened its mouth to receive your brother's blood from your hand.
12 When you work the ground, it will no longer give you its crops. You will be a runaway with no home on the earth."
13 But Cain said to the LORD, "My punishment is more than I can bear.
14 Look, today you have driven me away from this land. I will be hidden from your face. I will be a runaway with no home on the earth. Anyone who finds me will kill me."
15 The LORD replied, "Not so! If anyone kills Cain, Cain will be paid back seven times." Then the LORD put a mark on Cain. This way no one who found him would kill him.
16 So Cain went out from the presence of the LORD. He settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
17 Cain slept with his wife. She became pregnant and gave birth to Enoch. Then Cain built a city. He named it after his son Enoch.
18 Enoch was the father of Irad. Irad was the father of Mehujael. Mehujael was the father of Methusael. Methusael was the father of Lamech.
19 Lamech married two women. One was named Adah, the other Zillah.
20 Adah gave birth to Jabal. He was the father of people who live in tents and raise livestock.
21 His brother's name was Jubal. He was the father of all who play the harp and flute.
22 Zillah gave birth to Tubal-cain. He made all kinds of tools from bronze and iron. The sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.
23 Then Lamech said to his wives, "Adah and Zillah, hear my voice. Wives of Lamech, listen to my words. I have killed a man who hurt me, a young man who struck me.
24 If Cain is paid back seven times, then Lamech seventy-seven times."
25 Adam slept with his wife again. She gave birth to a son and named him Seth. She said, "God has given me another child in place of Abel, since Cain killed him."
26 A son was also born to Seth, and he named him Enosh. At that time people began to call on the name of the LORD.

title: Genesis 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 This is the record of Adam's family line. On the day God created man, he made him in the likeness of God.
2 He made them male and female. He blessed them. On the day they were created, he called them "man."
3 When Adam was 130 years old, he had a son in his own likeness, in his own image. He named him Seth.
4 After Seth was born, Adam lived 800 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
5 So Adam lived a total of 930 years, and then he died.
6 When Seth was 105 years old, he became the father of Enosh.
7 After Enosh was born, Seth lived 807 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
8 So Seth lived a total of 912 years, and then he died.
9 When Enosh was 90 years old, he became the father of Kenan.
10 After Kenan was born, Enosh lived 815 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
11 So Enosh lived a total of 905 years, and then he died.
12 When Kenan was 70 years old, he became the father of Mahalalel.
13 After Mahalalel was born, Kenan lived 840 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
14 So Kenan lived a total of 910 years, and then he died.
15 When Mahalalel was 65 years old, he became the father of Jared.
16 After Jared was born, Mahalalel lived 830 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
17 So Mahalalel lived a total of 895 years, and then he died.
18 When Jared was 162 years old, he became the father of Enoch.
19 After Enoch was born, Jared lived 800 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
20 So Jared lived a total of 962 years, and then he died.
21 When Enoch was 65 years old, he became the father of Methuselah.
22 After Methuselah was born, Enoch walked with God for 300 years. He had other sons and daughters.
23 So Enoch lived a total of 365 years.
24 Enoch walked with God. Then he was no more, because God took him away.
25 When Methuselah was 187 years old, he became the father of Lamech.
26 After Lamech was born, Methuselah lived 782 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
27 So Methuselah lived a total of 969 years, and then he died.
28 When Lamech was 182 years old, he had a son.
29 He named him Noah. He said, "May this one bring us comfort in the hard work of our hands, caused by the ground that the LORD has cursed."
30 After Noah was born, Lamech lived 595 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
31 So Lamech lived a total of 777 years, and then he died.
32 After Noah was 500 years old, he became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

title: Genesis 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 People began to grow in number on the earth. Daughters were born to them.
2 The sons of God saw that the daughters of men were beautiful. So they took as wives any women they chose.
3 Then the LORD said, "My Spirit will not stay with man forever. He is only flesh, and he will die. His days will be 120 years."

4 The Nephilim were on the earth in those days. They were there later too. This was when the sons of God had children by the daughters of men. Their children became the mighty men of old, the famous men.
5 Then the LORD saw that the evil of man was very great on the earth. Every plan in his heart was only evil all the time.
6 And the LORD was sorry that he had made man on the earth. His heart was filled with deep pain.
7 So the LORD said, "I will wipe away man, whom I made, from the face of the earth. I will wipe away every man and animal and small creature and bird of the sky. I am sorry that I made them."
8 But Noah found favor in the eyes of the LORD.
9 This is the story of Noah. Noah was a man right with God. He was without blame among the people of his time. Noah walked with God.
10 Noah had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
11 Now the earth was full of evil in the sight of God. It was full of violence.
12 God looked at the earth and saw that it was full of evil. All living things on the earth had ruined their ways.
13 Then God said to Noah, "The end of all living things has come before me. Through them the earth is full of violence. Now look — I will destroy them and the earth.
14 Make for yourself an ark of cypress wood. Make rooms in the ark. Cover it with tar inside and out.
15 This is how you are to build it. The ark must be 300 cubits long, 50 cubits wide, and 30 cubits high. (A cubit is about 18 inches.)
16 Make a roof for the ark. Finish its walls one cubit from the top. Put a door in the side of the ark. Build three decks: lower, middle, and upper.
17 Look — I will bring flood waters on the earth. I will destroy every creature under the sky that has the breath of life. Everything on the earth will die.
18 But I will set up my covenant with you. You will enter the ark — you, your sons, your wife, and your sons' wives with you.
19 Bring two of every kind of living thing into the ark — one male and one female. Keep them alive with you.
20 Two of every kind of bird, animal, and small creature will come to you. They will be kept alive.
21 Also take for yourself every kind of food that people eat. Store it as food for you and for the animals."
22 So Noah did everything exactly as God had commanded him.

title: Genesis 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Noah, "Go into the ark, you and all your family. I have seen that you are right with me among the people of this time.
2 Take with you seven pairs of every kind of clean animal — one male and one female. Also take one pair of every kind of unclean animal — one male and one female.
3 Take seven pairs of every kind of bird of the air, male and female. This will keep their kind alive on all the earth.
4 In seven days from now, I will send rain on the earth. It will rain for forty days and forty nights. I will wipe away from the face of the earth every living thing I have made."
5 And Noah did all that the LORD had commanded him.
6 Noah was 600 years old when the flood waters came on the earth.
7 Noah and his wife went into the ark. His sons and their wives went in with him. They went in to escape the waters of the flood.
8 The clean animals and the unclean animals, the birds, and every small animal that moves on the ground —
9 they came to Noah to enter the ark. They came two by two, male and female, just as God had commanded Noah.
10 And after seven days, the flood waters came on the earth.
11 It was the 600th year of Noah's life. On the seventeenth day of the second month, all the fountains of the great deep burst open. And the windows of heaven were opened.
12 The rain fell on the earth for forty days and forty nights.
13 On that very day, Noah went into the ark. His sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth went in with him. His wife and the three wives of his sons went in too.
14 They went in. And every kind of wild animal, farm animal, small animal that moves on the ground, bird, and winged creature went in too.
15 They came to Noah to enter the ark. They came two by two — every creature that has the breath of life.
16 The male and female of every living thing entered, just as God had commanded Noah. Then the LORD shut him in.
17 For forty days the flood kept coming on the earth. The waters rose and lifted the ark high above the earth.
18 The waters kept rising and grew very strong on the earth. The ark floated on the surface of the waters.
19 At last the waters completely covered the earth. All the high mountains under the whole sky were covered.
20 The waters rose and covered the mountaintops by fifteen cubits (about 22 feet).
21 Every living thing that moved on the earth died — birds, farm animals, wild animals, every small animal that moves on the earth, and all people.
22 Everything on dry land that had the breath of life in its nose died.

23 Every living thing on the face of the earth was destroyed — people and farm animals, small animals that move on the ground, and birds of the air. They were wiped away from the earth. Only Noah and those with him in the ark were left.
24 And the waters stayed strong on the earth for 150 days.

title: Genesis 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 But God remembered Noah and all the wild animals and farm animals that were with him in the ark. God sent a wind across the earth, and the waters began to go down.
2 The fountains of the deep and the windows of heaven were closed. The rain from the sky stopped.
3 The waters dropped steadily from the earth. After 150 days, the waters had gone down.
4 On the seventeenth day of the seventh month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
5 The waters kept going down until the tenth month. On the first day of the tenth month, the tops of the mountains could be seen.
6 After forty days, Noah opened the window he had made in the ark.
7 He sent out a raven. It kept flying back and forth until the waters had dried up from the earth.
8 Then Noah sent out a dove. He wanted to see if the waters had gone down from the ground.
9 But the dove found no place to rest her foot. So she came back to him in the ark. The waters were still covering all the earth. Noah reached out his hand and brought her back inside.
10 Noah waited seven more days. Then he sent out the dove again.
11 Look — the dove came back to him in the evening. In her beak was a fresh olive leaf. So Noah knew that the waters had gone down from the earth.
12 Noah waited seven more days and sent out the dove again. But this time she did not come back to him.
13 In Noah's six hundred and first year, on the first day of the first month, the waters had dried up from the earth. So Noah took the cover off the ark. He saw that the ground was dry.
14 By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was fully dry.
15 Then God said to Noah,
16 "Come out of the ark. Bring your wife, your sons, and your sons' wives.
17 Bring out every living thing with you — birds, farm animals, and every small animal that moves on the ground. Let them spread out over the earth. Let them have many young and grow in number."
18 So Noah came out, along with his sons, his wife, and his sons' wives.
19 Every living thing, every small animal that moves, and every bird — everything that moves on the earth — came out of the ark, each of its own kind.
20 Then Noah built an altar to the LORD. He took some of every clean animal and every clean bird. He offered burnt offerings on the altar.

21 When the LORD smelled the pleasing smell, he said in his heart, "Never again will I curse the ground because of man. Every thought of his heart is evil from when he is young. But never again will I destroy all living things, as I have done.
22 As long as the earth lasts, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night will never stop."

title: Genesis 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Then God blessed Noah and his sons. He said to them, "Have many children. Fill the earth.

2 All the animals on the earth will fear you. Every bird in the air will fear you. Every animal that crawls on the ground will fear you. All the fish of the sea will fear you too. I put them into your hand.
3 Everything that lives and moves will be food for you. I gave you the green plants. Now I give you all these things too.
4 But you must not eat meat with its life — that is, its blood — still in it.
5 And I will surely demand the life of any man or animal that sheds your blood. I will demand the life of anyone who kills another person.
6 Whoever sheds the blood of a person, his blood will be shed by people. For God made people in his own image.
7 As for you, have many children. Fill the earth and grow in number on it."
8 Then God said to Noah and his sons with him,
9 "Look, I now make my covenant with you and your children after you.
10 I make it with every living animal that was with you — the birds, the farm animals, and every wild animal. I make it with every living thing that came out of the ark.
11 I make my covenant with you. Never again will all life be destroyed by floodwaters. Never again will a flood destroy the earth."
12 And God said, "This is the sign of the covenant I am making. It is between me and you and every living animal with you. It is a covenant for all future generations.
13 I have set my rainbow in the clouds. It will be a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.
14 When I bring clouds over the earth, the rainbow will appear in the clouds.
15 Then I will remember my covenant. It is between me and you and every living animal of every kind. Never again will the waters become a flood that destroys all life.
16 When the rainbow appears in the clouds, I will see it. I will remember the covenant that lasts forever. It is between God and every living animal of every kind on the earth."
17 So God said to Noah, "This is the sign of the covenant. I have made it between me and every living thing on the earth."
18 The sons of Noah who came out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Ham was the father of Canaan.
19 These three were the sons of Noah. From them, the whole earth was filled with people.
20 Noah was a farmer. He began to plant a vineyard.
21 He drank some of its wine and became drunk. Then he uncovered his body inside his tent.
22 Ham, the father of Canaan, saw his father's naked body. He went outside and told his two brothers.

23 Then Shem and Japheth took a piece of cloth. They put it on their shoulders. They walked backward into the tent. They covered their father's naked body. Their faces were turned away. So they did not see their father's naked body.
24 When Noah woke up from his wine, he learned what his youngest son had done to him.
25 He said, "Cursed be Canaan! The lowest of slaves will he be to his brothers."
26 He also said, "Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem! May Canaan be the servant of Shem.
27 May God make the land of Japheth larger. May he live in the tents of Shem. And may Canaan be his servant."
28 After the flood, Noah lived 350 years.
29 So Noah lived a total of 950 years. Then he died.

title: Genesis 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 This is the record of Noah's sons — Shem, Ham, and Japheth. They had sons after the flood.
2 The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
3 The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
4 The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittim, and the Rodanim.
5 From these, the coastland peoples spread out into their lands. Each had its own language, by their clans, in their nations.
6 The sons of Ham: Cush, Egypt (Mizraim), Put, and Canaan.
7 The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
8 Cush was the father of Nimrod. Nimrod began to be a mighty man on the earth.
9 He was a mighty hunter before the LORD. So it is said, "Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the LORD."
10 His kingdom began in Babylon, Erech, Akkad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
11 From that land he went to Assyria. There he built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah,
12 and Resen, which lies between Nineveh and the great city of Calah.
13 Mizraim was the father of the Ludim, the Anamim, the Lehabim, the Naphtuhim,
14 the Pathrusim, the Casluhim (from whom the Philistines came), and the Caphtorim.
15 Canaan was the father of Sidon his firstborn, and of Heth,
16 the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
17 the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
18 the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. Later the Canaanite clans were scattered.
19 The borders of Canaan reached from Sidon toward Gerar all the way to Gaza. Then they went toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.
20 These are the sons of Ham, by their clans and languages, in their lands and nations.
21 Sons were also born to Shem, the older brother of Japheth. Shem was the father of all the sons of Eber.
22 The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram.
23 The sons of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
24 Arphaxad was the father of Shelah. Shelah was the father of Eber.
25 Two sons were born to Eber. One was named Peleg, because in his days the earth was divided. His brother was named Joktan.
26 Joktan was the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
27 Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
28 Obal, Abimael, Sheba,
29 Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were sons of Joktan.
30 Their land reached from Mesha toward Sephar, in the eastern hill country.
31 These are the sons of Shem, by their clans and languages, in their lands and nations.
32 These are the clans of Noah's sons, by their family lines and nations. From these the nations of the earth spread out after the flood.

title: Genesis 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Now the whole earth had one language and the same words.
2 As people traveled east, they found a flat land in Shinar. They settled there.
3 They said to one another, "Come, let us make bricks and bake them hard." So they used bricks instead of stone, and tar instead of mortar.
4 Then they said, "Come, let us build a city for ourselves. Let us build a tower whose top reaches the sky. Let us make a name for ourselves. Then we will not be scattered all over the earth."
5 Then the LORD came down to see the city and the tower that the people were building.
6 The LORD said, "Look — they are one people, and they all speak one language. This is only the beginning of what they will do. Now nothing they plan will be too hard for them.
7 Come, let us go down and mix up their language. Then they will not understand one another."
8 So the LORD scattered them from there all over the earth. And they stopped building the city.
9 That is why the city is called Babel. There the LORD mixed up the language of the whole earth. From that place the LORD scattered them all over the earth.
10 This is the record of Shem's family line. Two years after the flood, when Shem was 100 years old, he became the father of Arphaxad.
11 After Arphaxad was born, Shem lived 500 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
12 When Arphaxad was 35 years old, he became the father of Shelah.
13 After Shelah was born, Arphaxad lived 403 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
14 When Shelah was 30 years old, he became the father of Eber.
15 After Eber was born, Shelah lived 403 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
16 When Eber was 34 years old, he became the father of Peleg.
17 After Peleg was born, Eber lived 430 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
18 When Peleg was 30 years old, he became the father of Reu.
19 After Reu was born, Peleg lived 209 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
20 When Reu was 32 years old, he became the father of Serug.
21 After Serug was born, Reu lived 207 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
22 When Serug was 30 years old, he became the father of Nahor.
23 After Nahor was born, Serug lived 200 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
24 When Nahor was 29 years old, he became the father of Terah.
25 After Terah was born, Nahor lived 119 more years. He had other sons and daughters.
26 When Terah was 70 years old, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
27 This is the record of Terah's family line. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. And Haran became the father of Lot.
28 Haran died in the land where he was born, in Ur of the Chaldeans. His father Terah was still alive.
29 Abram and Nahor took wives for themselves. Abram's wife was named Sarai. Nahor's wife was named Milcah. She was the daughter of Haran, who was the father of both Milcah and Iscah.
30 But Sarai could not have children; she had no child.
31 Then Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his son's wife Sarai. They left Ur of the Chaldeans to go to the land of Canaan. But when they came to Haran, they stayed there.
32 Terah lived 205 years, and he died in Haran.

title: Genesis 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Abram, "Leave your country, your people, and your father's family. Go to the land I will show you.
2 I will make you into a great nation. I will bless you. I will make your name great. And you will be a blessing.
3 I will bless those who bless you. I will curse those who curse you. And all the families of the earth will be blessed through you."
4 So Abram left, just as the LORD had told him. Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he left Haran.
5 Abram took his wife Sarai and his nephew Lot. He took all the things and all the people they had gathered in Haran. They set out for the land of Canaan, and they arrived there.
6 Abram traveled through the land as far as Shechem. He came to the great tree of Moreh. At that time, the Canaanites lived in the land.
7 Then the LORD appeared to Abram. He said, "I will give this land to your offspring." So Abram built an altar there to the LORD, who had appeared to him.
8 From there Abram moved to the hills east of Bethel. He set up his tent, with Bethel to the west and Ai to the east. There he built an altar to the LORD. And he called on the name of the LORD.
9 Then Abram kept moving on toward the Negev.
10 There was a famine in the land. So Abram went down to Egypt to stay there for a while. The famine was very bad.
11 As he was about to enter Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, "Look, I know you are a beautiful woman.
12 When the Egyptians see you, they will say, 'This is his wife.' Then they will kill me but let you live.
13 Please say you are my sister. Then they will treat me well for your sake. My life will be spared because of you."
14 So when Abram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw that Sarai was very beautiful.
15 Pharaoh's officials saw her and spoke well of her to Pharaoh. So she was taken into Pharaoh's palace.
16 Pharaoh treated Abram well because of her. Abram gathered sheep and cattle, male and female donkeys, male and female servants, and camels.
17 But the LORD struck Pharaoh and his household with terrible diseases. This was because of Abram's wife Sarai.
18 So Pharaoh called Abram and asked, "What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me she was your wife?
19 Why did you say, 'She is my sister'? I took her to be my wife! Now then, here is your wife. Take her and go!"
20 Then Pharaoh gave his men orders about Abram. They sent him away with his wife and everything he owned.

title: Genesis 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 So Abram went up out of Egypt into the Negev. His wife and all that he owned went with him, and Lot too.
2 Abram had become very rich in animals, silver, and gold.
3 From the Negev, he moved from place to place toward Bethel. He came to the place between Bethel and Ai where his tent had been before,
4 the spot where he had built an altar. There Abram prayed to the LORD by name.
5 Lot, who was traveling with Abram, also had flocks, herds, and tents.
6 But the land could not support both of them together. They owned so many things that they could not live together in one place.
7 Fights broke out between Abram's herdsmen and Lot's herdsmen. At that time, the Canaanites and the Perizzites also lived in the land.
8 So Abram said to Lot, "Please, let there be no fighting between you and me. Let there be no fighting between your herdsmen and mine. After all, we are family.
9 The whole land is in front of you. Let's separate. If you go to the left, I will go to the right. If you go to the right, I will go to the left."

10 Lot lifted up his eyes and looked around. He saw that the whole Jordan Valley, all the way to Zoar, was full of water. It was like the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt. (This was before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.)
11 So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself. He set out toward the east. So Abram and Lot parted ways.
12 Abram lived in the land of Canaan. But Lot lived among the cities of the valley. He set up his tent close to Sodom.
13 Now the men of Sodom were very evil. They sinned greatly against the LORD.
14 After Lot had left, the LORD said to Abram, "Look up from where you are. Look north and south, east and west.
15 All the land that you see, I will give to you and your children forever.
16 I will make your children like the dust on the ground. If anyone could count the dust of the earth, then your children could be counted.
17 Get up and walk through the land. Go through all of it, for I am giving it to you."
18 So Abram moved his tent. He went to live near the great trees of Mamre at Hebron. There he built an altar for the LORD.

title: Genesis 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 In those days, four kings went to war. They were Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goiim.
2 They fought against five kings: Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar).
3 These last five kings joined together in the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Salt Sea).
4 For twelve years they had been under the rule of Chedorlaomer. But in the thirteenth year, they turned against him.
5 In the fourteenth year, Chedorlaomer and his allied kings went out. They defeated the Rephaites at Ashteroth-karnaim. They defeated the Zuzites at Ham, and the Emites at Shaveh-kiriathaim.
6 They also defeated the Horites in the area of Mount Seir, as far as El-paran, near the desert.
7 Then they turned back to attack En-mishpat (that is, Kadesh). They conquered the whole land of the Amalekites. They also defeated the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar.
8 Then the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bela (that is, Zoar) marched out. They lined up for battle in the Valley of Siddim.
9 They fought against Chedorlaomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goiim, Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar — four kings against five.
10 Now the Valley of Siddim was full of tar pits. As the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some men fell into the pits. The rest fled to the hill country.
11 The four kings took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah. They took all the food, and they went away.
12 They also took away Abram's nephew Lot and all he owned. Lot was living in Sodom at that time.
13 Then a man who escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew. Abram was living near the Oaks of Mamre the Amorite. Mamre was a brother of Eshcol and Aner. All three were partners with Abram by promise.
14 When Abram heard that his relative had been taken, he gathered the 318 trained men born in his house. They went after the enemy as far as Dan.
15 During the night, Abram divided his men into groups. He defeated Chedorlaomer's army. He chased them as far as Hobah, north of Damascus.
16 He brought back all the goods. He also brought back his relative Lot and all Lot owned. He brought back the women and the other people too.
17 Then Abram returned from defeating Chedorlaomer and the kings with him. The king of Sodom went out to meet him in the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King's Valley).
18 Then Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine. He was priest of God Most High.
19 He blessed Abram and said: "Blessed be Abram by God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
20 And blessed be God Most High, who has given your enemies into your hand." Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
21 The king of Sodom said to Abram, "Give me the people. But take the goods for yourself."
22 But Abram replied to the king of Sodom, "I have made a promise to the LORD God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
23 I will not accept even a thread or the strap of a sandal. I will take nothing that belongs to you. So you can never say, 'I have made Abram rich.'
24 I will accept nothing except what my men have eaten. The men who went with me — Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre — may take their share."

title: Genesis 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 After these things, the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision. He said, "Do not be afraid, Abram. I am your shield, your very great reward."
2 But Abram replied, "O Lord GOD, what can You give me? I still have no child. The one who will get all I have is Eliezer of Damascus."
3 Abram went on, "Look, You have given me no children. So a servant in my house will be my heir."
4 Then the word of the LORD came to Abram. He said, "This man will not be your heir. Your own son will be your heir."
5 The LORD took him outside and said, "Look up at the heavens. Count the stars, if you can." Then He told him, "So many will your children be."
6 Abram believed the LORD, and the LORD counted it to him as righteousness.
7 The LORD also said to him, "I am the LORD. I brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans. I will give you this land as your own."
8 But Abram replied, "Lord GOD, how can I know that I will have it?"
9 And the LORD said to him, "Bring Me a young cow, a goat, and a ram. Each must be three years old. Also bring a turtledove and a young pigeon."
10 So Abram brought all these to Him. He cut each one in half. He laid the halves across from each other. But he did not cut the birds in half.
11 Then wild birds came down on the dead animals. But Abram drove them away.
12 As the sun was going down, Abram fell into a deep sleep. Suddenly a great terror and a thick darkness came over him.
13 Then the LORD said to Abram, "Know this for sure: your descendants will be strangers in a land that is not their own. They will be made slaves and treated badly for four hundred years.
14 But I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. After that, they will leave with great wealth.
15 You, however, will go to your fathers in peace. You will be buried at a good old age.
16 In the fourth generation your descendants will come back here. For the sin of the Amorites has not yet reached its full measure."
17 When the sun had set and it was dark, look — a smoking firepot and a flaming torch appeared. They passed between the pieces of the dead animals.
18 On that day the LORD made a covenant with Abram. He said, "To your descendants I have given this land — from the river of Egypt to the great river, the Euphrates —
19 the land of the Kenites, Kenizzites, Kadmonites,
20 Hittites, Perizzites, Rephaites,
21 Amorites, Canaanites, Girgashites, and Jebusites."

title: Genesis 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Now Abram's wife Sarai had not given him any children. But she had an Egyptian servant whose name was Hagar.
2 So Sarai said to Abram, "Look, the LORD has kept me from having children. Please sleep with my servant. Maybe I can have a family through her." And Abram listened to what Sarai said.
3 Abram had lived in Canaan for ten years. Then his wife Sarai took her Egyptian servant Hagar and gave her to Abram to be his wife.
4 Abram slept with Hagar, and she became pregnant. But when Hagar realized she was pregnant, she began to look down on her mistress.

5 Then Sarai said to Abram, "You are responsible for the wrong done to me! I put my servant in your arms. Now that she knows she is pregnant, she looks down on me. May the LORD judge between you and me."
6 "Look," said Abram, "your servant is in your hands. Do whatever you want with her." Then Sarai treated Hagar so badly that Hagar ran away from her.
7 Now the angel of the LORD found Hagar by a spring of water in the desert. It was the spring beside the road to Shur.
8 "Hagar, servant of Sarai," he said, "where have you come from, and where are you going?" "I am running away from my mistress Sarai," she replied.
9 So the angel of the LORD told her, "Go back to your mistress and obey her."
10 Then the angel added, "I will give you many, many children. They will be too many to count."
11 The angel of the LORD went on: "Look, you are pregnant, and you will have a son. You will name him Ishmael, for the LORD has heard your cry of pain.
12 He will be a wild donkey of a man. His hand will be against everyone, and everyone's hand against him. He will live in fighting with all his brothers."
13 So Hagar gave this name to the LORD who had spoken to her: "You are the God who sees me." For she said, "I have now seen the One who sees me!"
14 That is why the well was called Beer-lahai-roi. It is between Kadesh and Bered.
15 So Hagar gave Abram a son. Abram gave the name Ishmael to the son she had given him.
16 Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar gave him Ishmael.

title: Genesis 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the LORD appeared to him. He said, "I am God Almighty. Walk before me and be blameless.
2 I will set up my covenant between me and you. I will greatly increase your numbers."
3 Then Abram fell facedown, and God said to him,
4 "As for me, this is my covenant with you: You will be the father of many nations.
5 No longer will you be called Abram. Your name will be Abraham, for I have made you a father of many nations.
6 I will make you very, very fruitful. I will make nations from you, and kings will come from you.
7 I will set up my covenant as an everlasting covenant between me and you. It will be for your children after you, too. I will be your God and the God of your children after you.
8 I will give you and your children the land where you are now living — all the land of Canaan. It will be yours forever. And I will be their God."
9 God also said to Abraham, "You must keep my covenant. So must your children in the years after you.
10 This is my covenant with you and your children after you. You must keep it: Every male among you must be circumcised.
11 You are to circumcise the flesh of your foreskin. This will be a sign of the covenant between me and you.

12 From now on, every male among you must be circumcised when he is eight days old. This includes those born in your house. It also includes those bought with money from a foreigner — even those who are not your own children.
13 Whether they are born in your house or bought, they must be circumcised. My covenant in your flesh will be an everlasting covenant.
14 But if any male is not circumcised, he will be cut off from his people. He has broken my covenant."
15 Then God said to Abraham, "As for Sarai your wife, do not call her Sarai. Her name will be Sarah.
16 I will bless her. I will surely give you a son by her. I will bless her so she will be the mother of nations. Kings of peoples will come from her."
17 Abraham fell facedown. He laughed and said to himself, "Can a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Can Sarah have a baby at the age of ninety?"
18 And Abraham said to God, "If only Ishmael could live under your blessing!"
19 But God replied, "Your wife Sarah will bear you a son. You will call him Isaac. I will set up my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his children after him.
20 As for Ishmael, I have heard you. I will surely bless him. I will make him fruitful and greatly increase his numbers. He will be the father of twelve rulers. I will make him into a great nation.
21 But my covenant I will set up with Isaac. Sarah will bear him to you by this time next year."
22 When God had finished speaking with Abraham, God went up from him.
23 That very day, Abraham took his son Ishmael. He also took every male born in his house and every male bought with his money. He circumcised them all, just as God had told him.
24 So Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised.
25 His son Ishmael was thirteen years old.
26 Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised on the same day.
27 All the men of Abraham's house were circumcised with him — both servants born in his house and those bought from foreigners.

title: Genesis 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD appeared to Abraham near the great trees of Mamre. It was the hottest part of the day. Abraham was sitting at the door of his tent.
2 Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. When he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them. He bowed down low to the ground.
3 "My lord," Abraham said, "if I have pleased you, please do not pass your servant by.
4 Let me bring a little water. You can wash your feet and rest under the tree.
5 I will bring some bread, so you can eat and feel strong again. After that, you may go on your way." "Yes," they replied, "do as you have said."
6 So Abraham hurried into the tent. He said to Sarah, "Quick! Take three large measures of fine flour. Mix it and bake some bread."
7 Then Abraham ran to the herd. He picked out a young and best calf. He gave it to a servant, who hurried to prepare it.
8 Then Abraham brought milk and curds (yogurt-like) and the cooked calf. He set the food before the men. He stood by them under the tree as they ate.
9 "Where is your wife Sarah?" they asked. "There, in the tent," he replied.
10 Then the LORD said, "I will surely come back to you about this time next year. And your wife Sarah will have a son!" Now Sarah was behind him, listening at the tent door.
11 Abraham and Sarah were already old. Sarah had passed the age when she could have children.
12 So she laughed inside herself. She said, "After I am old and tired, and my husband is old, will I really have this joy?"
13 Then the LORD asked Abraham, "Why did Sarah laugh? Why did she say, 'Can I really have a child when I am old?'
14 Is anything too hard for the LORD? At the set time next year I will come back to you. And Sarah will have a son."
15 Sarah was afraid, so she denied it. She said, "I did not laugh." But he said, "No, you did laugh."
16 Then the men got up to leave. They looked down toward Sodom. Abraham walked with them to see them off.
17 And the LORD said, "Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do?
18 Abraham will surely become a great and powerful nation. And through him all the nations of the earth will be blessed.
19 For I have chosen him. He will teach his children and his household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing what is right and just. Then the LORD will give Abraham what He has promised."
20 Then the LORD said, "The cry for help against Sodom and Gomorrah is great. Their sin is very bad.
21 I will go down and see if they are really doing what the cry says. If not, I will find out."
22 So the two men turned and went toward Sodom. But Abraham still stood before the LORD.
23 Abraham stepped forward and said, "Will You really wipe out the righteous with the wicked?
24 What if there are fifty righteous people in the city? Will You really wipe it out? Will You not save the place because of the fifty righteous ones there?
25 That would not be right for You! To kill the righteous with the wicked, so that they are treated the same — that would not be right! Will not the Judge of all the earth do what is right?"
26 So the LORD replied, "If I find fifty righteous people in the city of Sodom, I will save the whole place because of them."
27 Then Abraham answered, "Now I have dared to speak to the Lord, though I am only dust and ashes.
28 What if the fifty righteous ones are short by five? Will You destroy the whole city for the lack of five?" He replied, "If I find forty-five there, I will not destroy it."
29 Once again Abraham spoke to the LORD. "What if forty are found there?" He answered, "Because of the forty, I will not do it."
30 Then Abraham said, "Please do not be angry, Lord, but let me speak more. What if thirty are found there?" He replied, "If I find thirty there, I will not do it."
31 And Abraham said, "Now I have dared to speak to the Lord. What if twenty are found there?" He answered, "Because of the twenty, I will not destroy it."
32 Finally, Abraham said, "Please do not be angry, Lord, but let me speak once more. What if ten are found there?" And He answered, "Because of the ten, I will not destroy it."
33 When the LORD finished speaking with Abraham, He went away. And Abraham returned home.

title: Genesis 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 In the evening, the two angels came to Sodom. Lot was sitting at the city gate. When Lot saw them, he stood up to meet them. He bowed his face to the ground.
2 He said, "My lords, please come to your servant's house. Wash your feet and stay the night. Then you can get up early and go on your way." "No," they answered. "We will sleep in the open square."
3 But Lot begged them so hard that they went with him into his house. He made a meal for them and baked flat bread without yeast. And they ate.
4 Before they went to bed, all the men of the city of Sodom came. Both young and old surrounded the house.
5 They called out to Lot. They said, "Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, so that we may have sex with them!"
6 Lot went outside to meet them. He shut the door behind him.
7 "Please, my brothers," he begged, "do not do such a very evil thing!

8 Look, I have two daughters who have never been with a man. Let me bring them out to you. You can do to them as you please. But do not do anything to these men. They have come under the safety of my roof."

9 "Get out of the way!" they answered. They said, "This man came here as a foreigner. And now he is acting like our judge! We will treat you worse than them." Then they pressed hard against Lot. They moved in to break down the door.
10 But the men inside reached out. They pulled Lot into the house with them. Then they shut the door.
11 And they made the men at the door blind — both young and old. So the men got tired trying to find the door.
12 Then the two men said to Lot, "Do you have anyone else here? A son-in-law, your sons or daughters, or anyone else in the city who belongs to you? Get them out of here.
13 Because we are about to destroy this place. The cry of pain against its people is very great before the LORD. So the LORD has sent us to destroy this city."

14 So Lot went out. He spoke to the men who were going to marry his daughters. "Get up," he said. "Get out of this place. The LORD is about to destroy the city!" But his sons-in-law thought he was joking.
15 At daybreak, the angels hurried Lot along. They said, "Get up! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here. Or you will be killed when the city is punished."
16 But Lot waited too long. So the men grabbed his hand, his wife's hand, and the hands of his two daughters. They led them safely out of the city, because of the LORD's mercy for them.
17 As soon as the men had brought them out, one of them said, "Run to save your lives! Do not look back. Do not stop anywhere on the plain! Run to the mountains, or you will be killed!"
18 But Lot replied, "No, my lords, please!
19 Your servant has been shown great kindness by you. You have saved my life. But I cannot run to the mountains. The disaster will catch me, and I will die.
20 Look, there is a town nearby where I can run. It is a small place. Please let me run there. Is it not a small place? Then my life will be saved."
21 "Very well," he answered. "I will say yes to this request too. I will not destroy the town you point to.
22 Hurry! Run there quickly. For I cannot do anything until you reach it." That is why the town was called Zoar.
23 By the time the sun had risen over the land, Lot reached Zoar.
24 Then the LORD rained down burning sulfur and fire on Sodom and Gomorrah — from the LORD out of heaven.
25 Thus he destroyed those cities and the whole plain. He destroyed all the people living in the cities. He also destroyed the plants that grew on the ground.
26 But Lot's wife looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.
27 Early the next morning, Abraham got up. He returned to the place where he had stood before the LORD.
28 He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and all the land of the plain. He saw the smoke rising up from the land, like smoke from a hot oven.
29 So when God destroyed the cities of the plain, he remembered Abraham. He brought Lot out of the disaster that destroyed the cities where Lot had lived.
30 Lot and his two daughters left Zoar. They settled in the mountains, because Lot was afraid to stay in Zoar. They lived in a cave.
31 One day the older daughter said to the younger one, "Our father is old. There is no man in the land to sleep with us, as is the custom all over the earth.
32 Come, let us make our father drunk with wine. Then we can sleep with him and keep our family going."
33 So that night they made their father drunk with wine. The older daughter went in and slept with her father. He did not know when she lay down or when she got up.

34 The next day the older daughter said to the younger, "Look, I slept with my father last night. Let us make him drunk with wine again tonight. Then you can go in and sleep with him. So we can keep our father's family going."
35 So again that night they made their father drunk with wine. The younger daughter went in and slept with him. He did not know when she lay down or when she got up.
36 So both of Lot's daughters became pregnant by their father.
37 The older daughter gave birth to a son. She named him Moab. He is the father of the Moabites of today.
38 The younger daughter also gave birth to a son. She named him Ben-ammi. He is the father of the Ammonites of today.

title: Genesis 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Abraham traveled from there to the Negev region. He lived between Kadesh and Shur. He was staying in Gerar.
2 Abraham said about his wife Sarah, "She is my sister." So Abimelech king of Gerar sent for Sarah and took her.
3 But one night God came to Abimelech in a dream. God said, "You are about to die because of the woman you have taken. She is a married woman."
4 Now Abimelech had not gone near her. So he said, "Lord, will you destroy an innocent nation?
5 Didn't Abraham tell me, 'She is my sister'? And didn't she also say, 'He is my brother'? I have done this with a clear conscience and clean hands."
6 Then God said to him in the dream, "Yes, I know you did this with a clear conscience. That is why I stopped you from sinning against me. That is why I did not let you touch her.
7 Now give the man his wife back. He is a prophet, and he will pray for you, and you will live. But if you do not give her back, know for sure that you will die — you and all your people."
8 Early the next morning, Abimelech got up. He called all his servants together. He told them everything that had happened. The men were very afraid.

9 Then Abimelech called Abraham. He asked, "What have you done to us? How have I sinned against you? You have brought great guilt on me and my kingdom. You have done things to me that should not be done."
10 Abimelech also asked Abraham, "What made you do such a thing?"
11 Abraham answered, "I said to myself, 'Surely there is no fear of God in this place. They will kill me because of my wife.'
12 Besides, she really is my sister. She is the daughter of my father, but not the daughter of my mother. And she became my wife.
13 So when God had me leave my father's house, I said to Sarah, 'This is how you can be loyal to me. Wherever we go, say about me, "He is my brother."'"
14 So Abimelech brought sheep and cattle, and male and female slaves. He gave them to Abraham. He also gave his wife Sarah back to him.
15 And Abimelech said, "Look, my land is in front of you. Live wherever you want."
16 And he said to Sarah, "See, I am giving your brother 1,000 pieces of silver. This is proof that you are innocent before all who are with you. You are fully cleared."
17 Then Abraham prayed to God. And God healed Abimelech and his wife and his slave girls. So they could have children again.
18 For the LORD had closed every womb in Abimelech's household because of Abraham's wife Sarah.

title: Genesis 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Now the LORD was kind to Sarah, just as he had said. The LORD did for Sarah what he had promised.
2 So Sarah became pregnant. She gave birth to a son for Abraham in his old age. It happened at the very time God had promised.
3 Abraham gave the name Isaac to the son Sarah bore him.
4 When his son Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. He did this just as God had commanded him.
5 Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
6 Then Sarah said, "God has made me laugh. Everyone who hears about this will laugh with me."
7 She added, "Who would have told Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age."
8 The child grew and stopped drinking his mother's milk. On that day, Abraham held a great feast.
9 But Sarah saw something. The son that Hagar the Egyptian had borne to Abraham was making fun of her son.
10 So she said to Abraham, "Send this slave woman and her son away. That slave woman's son will never share in the inheritance with my son Isaac!"
11 This matter made Abraham very sad. It was about his son Ishmael.
12 But God said to Abraham, "Do not be so upset about the boy and your slave woman. Listen to whatever Sarah tells you. For through Isaac your family will be counted.
13 I will also make a nation from the slave woman's son, because he is your offspring."
14 Early in the morning, Abraham got up. He took some bread and a skin of water. He put them on Hagar's shoulders and sent her away with the boy. She left and wandered in the Desert of Beersheba.
15 When the water in the skin was all gone, she left the boy under one of the bushes.
16 Then she went off and sat down nearby, as far as an arrow can fly. She said, "I cannot watch the boy die." And as she sat there, she began to cry out loud.
17 Then God heard the boy's voice. The angel of God called to Hagar from heaven. He said, "What is wrong, Hagar? Do not be afraid. God has heard the boy's voice where he lies.
18 Get up. Lift up the boy and take him by the hand. For I will make him into a great nation."
19 Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. So she went and filled the skin with water. She gave the boy a drink.
20 God was with the boy. He grew up and lived in the desert. He became very good with a bow and arrow.
21 While he was living in the Desert of Paran, his mother got a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
22 At that time, King Abimelech came to Abraham. Phicol, the commander of his army, came with him. They said, "God is with you in all that you do.

23 Now then, promise me here before God. Promise that you will not lie to me, or to my children, or to my descendants. Show me the same kindness that I have shown to you. Do the same to the country where you are living."
24 And Abraham replied, "I promise."
25 Then Abraham complained to Abimelech. Some of Abimelech's servants had taken a well from him.
26 Abimelech replied, "I do not know who has done this. You did not tell me. I have not heard about it until today."
27 So Abraham brought sheep and cattle and gave them to Abimelech. The two men made a covenant.
28 Abraham set seven female lambs apart from the flock.
29 Abimelech asked him, "Why have you set apart these seven female lambs?"
30 He replied, "You must accept the seven female lambs from my hand. They will be my witness that I dug this well."
31 So that place was called Beersheba. There the two of them made a promise before God.
32 After they had made the covenant at Beersheba, Abimelech and Phicol got up. They returned to the land of the Philistines.
33 Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba. There he called on the name of the LORD, the Eternal God.
34 And Abraham lived in the land of the Philistines for a long time.

title: Genesis 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Some time later, God tested Abraham. He said to him, "Abraham!" "Here I am," he answered.
2 "Take your son," God said, "your only son, whom you love — Isaac — and go to the region of Moriah. Sacrifice him there as a burnt offering on a mountain I will show you."

3 Early the next morning, Abraham got up. He put a saddle on his donkey. He took two of his servants and his son Isaac with him. He cut the wood for the burnt offering. Then he set out for the place God had chosen.
4 On the third day, Abraham looked up. He saw the place far away.
5 Abraham said to his servants, "Stay here with the donkey. The boy and I will go over there. We will worship, and then we will come back to you."
6 Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering. He placed it on his son Isaac. He himself carried the fire and the knife. And the two of them walked on together.
7 Then Isaac said to his father Abraham, "My father?" "Yes, my son?" he replied. "The fire and the wood are here," said Isaac, "but where is the lamb for the burnt offering?"
8 Abraham answered, "God himself will provide the lamb for the burnt offering, my son." And the two walked on together.
9 They came to the place God had chosen. Abraham built the altar there and laid out the wood. He tied up his son Isaac and placed him on the altar, on top of the wood.
10 Then Abraham reached out his hand. He took the knife to kill his son.
11 Just then, the angel of the LORD called out to him from heaven. "Abraham! Abraham!" "Here I am," he replied.
12 "Do not lay a hand on the boy," he said. "Do not do anything to him. Now I know that you fear God, because you have not kept back your son, your only son, from me."
13 Then Abraham looked up. Behind him he saw a ram caught by its horns in a thicket. So he went and took the ram. He offered it as a burnt offering instead of his son.
14 So Abraham called that place "The LORD Will Provide." And to this day it is said, "On the mountain of the LORD it will be provided."
15 The angel of the LORD called to Abraham from heaven a second time.
16 He said, "I swear by myself, declares the LORD, that because you have done this and have not kept back your son, your only son,
17 I will surely bless you. I will make your descendants very many — like the stars in the sky and the sand on the seashore. Your descendants will take the cities of their enemies.
18 And through your offspring all nations on earth will be blessed, because you have obeyed me."
19 Then Abraham went back to his servants. They got up and set out together for Beersheba. And Abraham settled in Beersheba.
20 Some time later, someone told Abraham, "Milcah has also given birth to sons. They are for your brother Nahor:
21 Uz the firstborn, his brother Buz, Kemuel (the father of Aram),
22 Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel."
23 And Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. Milcah bore these eight sons to Abraham's brother Nahor.
24 Also, Nahor's second wife, whose name was Reumah, bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.

title: Genesis 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Sarah lived 127 years. That was the length of her life.
2 She died at Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan. Abraham went in to mourn and weep for her.
3 Then Abraham got up from beside his dead wife. He spoke to the Hittites.
4 "I am a foreigner and sojourner among you," he said. "Sell me some property for a burial site. Then I can bury my dead."
5 The Hittites answered Abraham,
6 "Listen to us, sir. You are a mighty prince among us. Bury your dead in the best of our tombs. None of us will keep back his tomb from you."
7 Then Abraham stood up and bowed down before the people of the land, the Hittites.
8 "If you are willing for me to bury my dead," he said, "then listen to me. Ask Ephron son of Zohar for me.
9 Ask him to sell me the cave of Machpelah, which belongs to him. It is at the end of his field. Let him sell it to me in front of you for the full price. Then I will have a burial site."
10 Now Ephron was sitting among the sons of Heth. So in front of all the Hittites who had come to the gate of the city, Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham,
11 "No, my lord. Listen to me. I give you the field. I also give you the cave that is in it. I give it to you in front of my people. Bury your dead."
12 Again Abraham bowed down before the people of the land.
13 He said to Ephron in front of them, "Please listen to me. Let me pay the price of the field. Accept it from me. Then I will bury my dead there."
14 Ephron answered Abraham,
15 "Listen to me, my lord. The land is worth 400 shekels of silver. But what is that between you and me? Go and bury your dead."
16 Abraham agreed to Ephron's terms. He weighed out for him the price he had named, in the hearing of the Hittites: 400 shekels of silver, according to the weight that merchants used.
17 So Ephron's field at Machpelah near Mamre — the field, and the cave that was in it, and all the trees within the edges of the field —
18 were given over by law to Abraham as his property. This was done in front of all the Hittites who had come to the gate of the city.
19 After this, Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave of the field at Machpelah near Mamre (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan.
20 So the field and the cave in it were given over by the Hittites to Abraham as a burial site.

title: Genesis 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 By now Abraham was very old. The LORD had blessed him in every way.
2 Abraham said to the head servant of his house, who took care of all he owned, "Put your hand under my thigh.
3 I want you to swear by the LORD, the God of heaven and the God of earth. You must not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites. I am living among them now.
4 Instead, you must go to my country and to my relatives. There you will get a wife for my son Isaac."
5 The servant asked him, "What if the woman is unwilling to come back with me to this land? Should I then take your son back to the land you came from?"
6 Abraham replied, "Make sure you do not take my son back there.

7 The LORD, the God of heaven, brought me from my father's house and my native land. He spoke to me and promised on oath, 'I will give this land to your offspring.' He will send his angel ahead of you. So you will get a wife for my son from there.
8 And if the woman is unwilling to come with you, then you are free from this oath. Only do not take my son back there."
9 So the servant put his hand under the thigh of his master Abraham. He swore an oath to him about this matter.
10 Then the servant took ten of his master's camels. He set out with all kinds of good things from his master. He went to Nahor's hometown in Aram-naharaim.
11 Evening was near when he arrived. He made the camels kneel down by the well outside the town. It was the time when women came out to get water.
12 Then he prayed, "O LORD, God of my master Abraham, please give me success today. Show kindness to my master Abraham.
13 Here I am, standing by the spring. The daughters of the townspeople are coming out to get water.

14 May it be like this: I will say to a young woman, 'Please let down your jar so I may have a drink.' And she will say, 'Drink, and I will water your camels too.' Let her be the one you have chosen for your servant Isaac. By this I will know you have shown kindness to my master."
15 Before the servant had finished praying, Rebekah came out. She had her jar on her shoulder. She was the daughter of Bethuel, the son of Milcah. Milcah was the wife of Abraham's brother Nahor.
16 The young woman was very beautiful. No man had ever known her. She went down to the spring, filled her jar, and came up again.
17 The servant ran to meet her. "Please let me have a little water from your jar," he said.
18 "Drink, my lord," she replied. She quickly lowered her jar to her hands and gave him a drink.
19 After he had finished drinking, she said, "I will also get water for your camels. I will draw until they have had enough."
20 She quickly emptied her jar into the water trough. Then she ran back to the well to get more water. She drew water for all his camels.
21 Meanwhile, the man watched her silently. He wanted to see if the LORD had made his journey a success.
22 When the camels had finished drinking, the man took out a gold ring. It weighed a half-shekel. He also took out two gold bracelets for her wrists. They weighed ten shekels.
23 "Whose daughter are you?" he asked. "Please tell me. Is there room in your father's house for us to spend the night?"
24 She replied, "I am the daughter of Bethuel, the son that Milcah bore to Nahor."
25 Then she added, "We have plenty of straw and feed for the camels. We also have a place for you to spend the night."
26 Then the man bowed down and worshiped the LORD.

27 He said, "Praise be to the LORD, the God of my master Abraham. He has not held back his kindness and faithfulness from my master. The LORD has led me on the journey to the house of my master's relatives."
28 The young woman ran and told her mother's household about these things.
29 Now Rebekah had a brother named Laban. He rushed out to the man at the spring.

30 Laban saw the ring and the bracelets on his sister's wrists. He heard Rebekah's words, "The man said this to me." So he went out and found the man. The man was standing by the camels near the spring.
31 "Come in, you who are blessed by the LORD," said Laban. "Why are you standing out here? I have prepared the house and a place for the camels."
32 So the man came to the house. The camels were unloaded. Straw and feed were brought to the camels. Water was brought to wash his feet and the feet of his men.
33 Then a meal was set before the man. But he said, "I will not eat until I have told you what I came to say." "Please speak," said Laban.
34 "I am Abraham's servant," he said.
35 "The LORD has greatly blessed my master. He has become rich. The LORD has given him sheep and cattle, silver and gold. He has given him menservants and maidservants, camels and donkeys.
36 My master's wife Sarah bore him a son in her old age. My master has given him everything he owns.
37 My master made me swear an oath. He said, 'You must not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites. I live in their land.
38 Instead, you must go to my father's house and to my relatives. There you will get a wife for my son.'
39 Then I asked my master, 'What if the woman will not come back with me?'

40 He answered, 'The LORD, before whom I have walked, will send his angel with you. He will make your journey a success. So you will get a wife for my son from my relatives and from my father's house.
41 When you go to my relatives, if they refuse to give her to you, then you will be free from my oath.'
42 So today I came to the spring. I prayed, 'O LORD, God of my master Abraham, please make my journey a success!
43 Here I am, standing by this spring. Now a young woman will come out to get water. I will say to her, "Please let me drink a little water from your jar."
44 She will reply, "Drink, and I will get water for your camels too." Let her be the woman the LORD has chosen for my master's son.'
45 Before I had finished praying in my heart, there was Rebekah. She came out with her jar on her shoulder. She went down to the spring and got water. So I said to her, 'Please give me a drink.'
46 She quickly lowered her jar from her shoulder. She said, 'Drink, and I will water your camels too.' So I drank, and she also watered the camels.
47 Then I asked her, 'Whose daughter are you?' She replied, 'The daughter of Bethuel son of Nahor, whom Milcah bore to him.' So I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her wrists.
48 Then I bowed down and worshiped the LORD. I praised the LORD, the God of my master Abraham. He had led me on the right road. He let me take the granddaughter of my master's brother for his son.
49 Now please tell me: will you show kindness and faithfulness to my master? If not, let me know. Then I may turn another way."
50 Laban and Bethuel answered, "This is from the LORD. We cannot say anything for or against it.
51 Rebekah is here before you. Take her and go. Let her become the wife of your master's son, just as the LORD has said."
52 When Abraham's servant heard their words, he bowed down to the ground before the LORD.
53 Then he brought out silver and gold jewels and beautiful clothing. He gave them to Rebekah. He also gave costly gifts to her brother and her mother.
54 Then he and the men with him ate and drank. They spent the night there. When they got up the next morning, he said, "Let me go on my way to my master."
55 But her brother and mother said, "Let the young woman stay with us ten days or so. After that, she may go."
56 But he replied, "Do not delay me. The LORD has made my journey a success. Let me go on my way to my master."
57 So they said, "We will call the young woman and ask her."
58 They called Rebekah and asked her, "Will you go with this man?" "I will go," she replied.
59 So they sent their sister Rebekah on her way. Her nurse went with her, and Abraham's servant and his men also went.
60 They blessed Rebekah. They said to her, "Our sister, may you become the mother of thousands upon thousands. May your children take the city gates of their enemies."
61 Then Rebekah and her servant girls got ready. They mounted the camels and followed the man. So the servant took Rebekah and left.
62 Now Isaac had just returned from Beer-lahai-roi. He was living in the Negev.
63 Early in the evening, Isaac went out to the field to think quietly. He looked up and saw the camels coming.
64 When Rebekah looked up and saw Isaac, she got down from her camel.
65 She asked the servant, "Who is that man in the field coming to meet us?" "It is my master," the servant answered. So she took her veil and covered herself.
66 Then the servant told Isaac everything he had done.
67 Isaac brought Rebekah into the tent of his mother Sarah. He took Rebekah as his wife. Isaac loved her, and he was comforted after his mother's death.

title: Genesis 25
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Abraham took another wife. Her name was Keturah.
2 She gave him six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
3 Jokshan was the father of Sheba and Dedan. The sons of Dedan were the Asshurites, the Letushites, and the Leummites.
4 The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.
5 Abraham left everything he owned to Isaac.
6 But while he was still alive, Abraham gave gifts to the sons of his other wives. Then he sent them away from his son Isaac, to the land of the east.
7 Abraham lived 175 years in all.
8 He died when he was very old, after a full life. And he was gathered to his people.
9 His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him together. They buried him in the cave of Machpelah, near Mamre. The cave was in the field of Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite.
10 Abraham had bought this field from the Hittites. He was buried there with his wife Sarah.
