24 The servant took the men into Joseph's house. He gave them water to wash their feet. He also gave food to their donkeys.
25 The brothers had been told they would eat there. So they got their gift ready for Joseph's arrival at noon.
26 When Joseph came home, they gave him the gifts they had brought. They bowed down to the ground before him.
27 He asked if they were well. Then he asked, "How is your aged father you told me about? Is he still alive?"
28 "Your servant our father is well," they answered. "He is still alive." Then they bowed down to honor him.
29 Joseph looked up and saw his brother Benjamin, his own mother's son. He asked, "Is this your youngest brother, the one you told me about?" Then he said, "God be gracious to you, my son."
30 Joseph hurried out. He was so full of feeling for his brother that he had to cry. He went to a room by himself to weep.
31 Then he washed his face and came back out. He got calm again. He said, "Serve the meal."
32 They served Joseph by himself, the brothers by themselves, and the Egyptians by themselves. The Egyptians would not eat with the Hebrews, because that was hated by them.
33 The brothers were seated before Joseph in order by age — from the oldest to the youngest. The men looked at one another in shock.
34 Portions were served to them from Joseph's table. But Benjamin's portion was five times as much as any of the others. So they ate and drank freely with Joseph.

title: Genesis 44
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Then Joseph told his head servant, "Fill the men's sacks with all the food they can carry. Put each man's silver in the mouth of his sack.
2 Put my cup, the silver one, in the mouth of the youngest one's sack. Put his silver for the grain there too." So the head servant did as Joseph said.
3 At dawn, the men were sent on their way with their donkeys.
4 They had not gone far from the city when Joseph spoke to his head servant. "Go after the men at once," he said. "When you catch up with them, ask, 'Why have you paid back good with evil?
5 Is this not the cup my master drinks from? He even uses it for divination! What you have done is very wrong.'"
6 When the head servant caught up with them, he said these words to them.
7 "Why does my lord say such things?" they asked. "We would never do such a thing.
8 We even brought back to you from the land of Canaan the silver we found in the mouths of our sacks. So why would we steal silver or gold from your master's house?
9 If any of your servants is found to have it, he must die. And the rest of us will become my lord's slaves."
10 "Very well," said the head servant. "Let it be as you say. But only the one who is found with the cup will be my slave. The rest of you will be free from blame."
11 So each man quickly lowered his sack to the ground. Each man opened his sack.
12 The head servant searched. He began with the oldest and ended with the youngest. And the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
13 At this, they tore their clothes. They loaded their donkeys and went back to the city.
14 Judah and his brothers arrived at Joseph's house. He was still there. They fell to the ground before him.
15 "What is this you have done?" Joseph said. "Do you not know that a man like me can surely find out the truth by divination?"

16 "What can we say to my lord?" Judah replied. "What can we say? How can we prove ourselves innocent? God has uncovered your servants' guilt. We are now my lord's slaves — we ourselves and the one who was found with the cup."
17 But Joseph replied, "I would never do that. Only the man who was found with the cup will be my slave. The rest of you, go back to your father in peace."
18 Then Judah came close to Joseph and said, "Please, my lord, let your servant speak a word to my lord. Do not be angry with your servant, for you are equal to Pharaoh himself.
19 My lord asked his servants, 'Do you have a father or a brother?'

20 And we answered my lord, 'We have an old father. And we have a younger brother, a son born when he was old. The boy's brother is dead. He is the only one of his mother's sons left. And his father loves him.'
21 Then you told your servants, 'Bring him down to me, so that I can see him for myself.'
22 So we said to my lord, 'The boy cannot leave his father. If he leaves, his father will die.'
23 But you said to your servants, 'Unless your younger brother comes down with you, you will not see my face again.'
24 Now when we returned to your servant my father, we told him your words.
25 Then our father said, 'Go back and buy us some food.'
26 But we answered, 'We cannot go down there unless our younger brother goes with us. If our younger brother is not with us, we cannot see the man.'
27 And your servant my father said to us, 'You know that my wife bore me two sons.
28 One of them went away from me, and I said, "He has surely been torn to pieces." And I have not seen him since.
29 Now if you also take this one from me and harm comes to him, you will bring my gray head down to the grave in sorrow.'
30 So now, if I return to your servant my father, and the boy is not with us — for his life is tied to the boy's life —
31 when he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die. Then your servants will have brought the gray head of your servant our father down to the grave in sorrow.
32 Your servant promised to keep the boy safe for my father. I said, 'If I do not bring him back to you, I will carry the blame before you, my father, all my life.'
33 Now then, please let your servant stay here as my lord's slave in place of the boy. Let the boy return with his brothers.
34 For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? No! Do not let me see the misery that would come on my father."

title: Genesis 45
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Then Joseph could not hold back any longer in front of all his servants. He cried out, "Have everyone leave my presence!" So no one was with Joseph when he made himself known to his brothers.
2 But he cried so loudly that the Egyptians heard him. And Pharaoh's house soon heard about it.
3 Joseph said to his brothers, "I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?" But they could not answer him. They were so afraid in front of him.
4 Then Joseph said to his brothers, "Please come close to me." And they did. "I am Joseph, your brother," he said, "the one you sold into Egypt!
5 And now, do not be upset or angry with yourselves for selling me here. Because it was to save lives that God sent me ahead of you.
6 The famine has covered the land for two years now. And there will be five more years without plowing or harvesting.
7 God sent me ahead of you to keep a remnant of you alive on the earth. He sent me to save your lives by a great rescue.
8 So then, it was not you who sent me here, but God. He has made me like a father to Pharaoh — lord of all his house, and ruler over all the land of Egypt.
9 Now return quickly to my father. Tell him, 'This is what your son Joseph says: God has made me lord of all Egypt. Come down to me without delay.
10 You will live in the land of Goshen and be near me — you and your children and grandchildren, your sheep and cattle, and everything you own.
11 And there I will take care of you, because there will be five more years of famine. If not, you and your house and everything you own will lose everything.'
12 Look! You and my brother Benjamin can see that I, Joseph, am the one speaking with you.
13 Tell my father about all my honor in Egypt and everything you have seen. And bring my father down here quickly."
14 Then Joseph hugged his brother Benjamin and wept. And Benjamin wept as they held each other.
15 Joseph kissed each of his brothers as he wept over them. And after that his brothers talked with him.
16 The news reached Pharaoh's house that Joseph's brothers had come. Pharaoh and his servants were pleased.
17 Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Tell your brothers, 'Do this: Load your animals and return to the land of Canaan.
18 Then bring your father and your families and come back to me. I will give you the best of the land of Egypt. You will eat the best food of the land.'
19 You are also told to tell them: 'Take carts from the land of Egypt for your young children and your wives. Bring your father and come back.
20 But do not worry about your things, because the best of all the land of Egypt is yours.'"
21 So the sons of Israel did as they were told. Joseph gave them carts as Pharaoh had instructed. He also gave them food for the trip.
22 He gave new clothes to each of them. But to Benjamin he gave 300 pieces of silver and five sets of clothes.
23 And he sent this to his father: ten male donkeys loaded with the best things of Egypt, and ten female donkeys loaded with grain and bread and food for his father's trip.
24 Then Joseph sent his brothers on their way. As they were leaving, he said to them, "Do not quarrel on the way!"
25 So the brothers went up out of Egypt. They came to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan.
26 "Joseph is still alive," they said, "and he is ruler over all the land of Egypt!" But Jacob was shocked, because he did not believe them.
27 However, they told him all that Joseph had said. And he saw the carts that Joseph had sent to carry him back. Then the spirit of their father Jacob came back to life.
28 "I'm convinced!" said Israel. "My son Joseph is still alive! I will go to see him before I die."

title: Genesis 46
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 So Israel left with everything he had. When he came to Beersheba, he offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
2 That night God spoke to Israel in a vision. "Jacob! Jacob!" he called. "Here I am," Jacob replied.
3 "I am God, the God of your father," he said. "Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt. There I will make you into a great nation.
4 I will go down to Egypt with you. And I will surely bring you back again. Joseph's own hands will close your eyes."
5 Then Jacob left Beersheba. The sons of Israel carried their father Jacob in the carts Pharaoh had sent. They also brought their children and their wives.
6 They took the farm animals and the things they had gathered in Canaan. Jacob and all his family went to Egypt.
7 Jacob took to Egypt his sons and grandsons, his daughters and granddaughters — all his family.
8 These are the names of the sons of Israel — Jacob and his family — who went to Egypt: Reuben, Jacob's firstborn.
9 The sons of Reuben: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
10 The sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul. Shaul was the son of a Canaanite woman.
11 The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
12 The sons of Judah: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah. But Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan. The sons of Perez: Hezron and Hamul.
13 The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puvah, Job, and Shimron.
14 The sons of Zebulun: Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
15 These are the sons of Leah, born to Jacob in Paddan-aram. There was also his daughter Dinah. The total of his sons and daughters was thirty-three.
16 The sons of Gad: Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
17 The children of Asher: Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah. The sons of Beriah: Heber and Malchiel.
18 These are the sons of Jacob born to Zilpah. Laban had given Zilpah to his daughter Leah. They were sixteen in all.
19 The sons of Jacob's wife Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.
20 Manasseh and Ephraim were born to Joseph in the land of Egypt. Their mother was Asenath, daughter of Potiphera, priest of On.
21 The sons of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.
22 These are the sons of Rachel born to Jacob — fourteen in all.
23 The son of Dan: Hushim.
24 The sons of Naphtali: Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
25 These are the sons of Jacob born to Bilhah. Laban had given Bilhah to his daughter Rachel — seven in all.
26 All the people who came to Egypt with Jacob were his own children. They did not count the wives of Jacob's sons. They were sixty-six people in all.
27 Two sons had been born to Joseph in Egypt. So Jacob's family in Egypt was seventy in total.
28 Jacob had sent Judah ahead to Joseph. Judah was to ask the way to Goshen. So they came to the land of Goshen.
29 Joseph got his chariot ready and went up to meet his father Israel. As soon as Joseph appeared before him, he threw his arms around his father. He wept for a long time.
30 Then Israel said to Joseph, "Now I am ready to die. I have seen your face myself. I know you are still alive!"
31 Joseph said to his brothers and his father's family, "I will go up and tell Pharaoh. I will say, 'My brothers and my father's family have come from Canaan to me.
32 The men are shepherds. They take care of farm animals. They have brought their flocks and herds and all they own.'
33 When Pharaoh calls you, he will ask, 'What is your job?'

34 Then you must say, 'Your servants have raised farm animals since we were young. Our fathers did the same.' Then he will let you live in the land of Goshen. For all shepherds are hated by the Egyptians."

title: Genesis 47
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Joseph went and told Pharaoh, "My father and my brothers have come from Canaan. They brought their sheep, their cattle, and all they own. They are now in Goshen."
2 Joseph chose five of his brothers and brought them before Pharaoh.
3 Pharaoh asked the brothers, "What is your work?" They answered, "Your servants are shepherds, just as our fathers were."

4 They said to Pharaoh, "We have come to live in this land for a time. The famine in Canaan is very severe. There is no grass for the animals of your servants. So please let us settle in the land of Goshen."
5 Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Your father and your brothers have come to you.

6 The land of Egypt is before you. Settle your father and your brothers in the best of the land. Let them live in Goshen. And if you know of any among them with special ability, put them in charge of my own animals."
7 Then Joseph brought in his father Jacob and presented him before Pharaoh. And Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
8 Pharaoh asked Jacob, "How old are you?"
9 Jacob answered, "The years of my pilgrimage are 130. My years have been few and difficult. They do not equal the years of the pilgrimage of my fathers."
10 Then Jacob blessed Pharaoh and went out from his presence.
11 So Joseph settled his father and his brothers in Egypt. He gave them land in the best part of the country, in the district of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.
12 Joseph also gave food to his father, his brothers, and all his father's household. He gave food for each of their families.
13 There was no food in all that region. The famine was so severe that Egypt and Canaan were used up by it.
14 Joseph collected all the silver in Egypt and Canaan. The people gave it for grain. He brought the silver into Pharaoh's palace.
15 When the silver was gone, all the Egyptians came to Joseph. They said, "Give us food! Why should we die in front of you? Our silver is all used up."
16 Joseph said, "Bring me your animals. Since the silver is gone, I will give you food in exchange for them."
17 So they brought their animals to Joseph. He gave them food for their horses, sheep, goats, cattle, and donkeys. All that year he fed them in exchange for their animals.

18 When that year was over, they came to him the next year. They said, "We cannot hide from our lord that our silver is gone. All our animals belong to you. Nothing is left for our lord except our bodies and our land.

19 Why should we die before your eyes — we and our land? Buy us and our land in exchange for food. Then we and our land will be in bondage to Pharaoh. Give us seed so we may live and not die. Then the land will not become empty."
20 So Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh. Each Egyptian sold his field because the famine was so severe. The land became Pharaoh's.
21 Joseph moved the people into the cities, from one end of Egypt to the other.
22 But he did not buy the land of the priests. Pharaoh gave them a food share, so they did not need to sell their land.
23 Then Joseph said to the people, "Today I have bought you and your land for Pharaoh. Here is seed for you to plant in the land.
24 At harvest time, you shall give a fifth of it to Pharaoh. The other four-fifths you may keep as seed for the fields and as food for yourselves and your households and your children."
25 They said, "You have saved our lives. May we find favor in the eyes of our lord. We will be in bondage to Pharaoh."
26 So Joseph established it as a law concerning land in Egypt — still in force today — that a fifth of the produce belongs to Pharaoh. Only the land of the priests did not belong to Pharaoh.
27 Now the Israelites settled in Egypt, in the region of Goshen. They got property there. They became very fruitful and grew many times in number.
28 Jacob lived in Egypt seventeen years. The whole length of his life was 147 years.

29 When the time drew near for Israel to die, he called his son Joseph. He said, "If I have found favor in your eyes, put your hand under my thigh. Promise to show me kindness and faithfulness. Do not bury me in Egypt.
30 When I rest with my fathers, carry me out of Egypt and bury me where they are buried." Joseph said, "I will do as you say."
31 Jacob said, "Swear to me." So Joseph swore to him. Then Israel worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.

title: Genesis 48
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Some time later, someone told Joseph, "Your father is ill." So Joseph took his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim, and went to him.
2 When Jacob was told, "Your son Joseph has come to you," Israel gathered his strength and sat up in bed.
3 Jacob said to Joseph, "God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan. There he blessed me.
4 He said to me, 'Look, I will make you fruitful and increase your numbers. I will make you a community of peoples. I will give this land to your descendants after you as an everlasting possession.'
5 Now then, your two sons born to you in Egypt before I came here will be counted as mine. Ephraim and Manasseh will be mine, just as Reuben and Simeon are mine.
6 Any children born to you after them will be yours. They will be called by the names of their brothers in the land they inherit.

7 As for me, when I was returning from Paddan, with deep sorrow Rachel died in the land of Canaan. We were still on the way, a little distance from Ephrath. So I buried her there beside the road to Ephrath" (that is, Bethlehem).
8 When Israel saw the sons of Joseph, he asked, "Who are these?"
9 Joseph said to his father, "They are the sons God has given me in this place." So Jacob said, "Please bring them to me, so I may bless them."
10 Now Israel's eyes were weak because of old age; he could hardly see. Joseph brought his sons close to him. His father kissed them and hugged them.
11 Israel said to Joseph, "I never expected to see your face again. But now God has even let me see your children too."
12 Then Joseph took his sons from his father's knees and bowed with his face to the ground.
13 Joseph took both of them. He put Ephraim in his right hand toward Israel's left hand. He put Manasseh in his left hand toward Israel's right hand. Then he brought them close to him.
14 But Israel reached out his right hand and put it on the head of Ephraim, the younger. Crossing his arms, he put his left hand on Manasseh's head, even though Manasseh was the firstborn.
15 Then he blessed Joseph and said: "May the God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, the God who has been my shepherd all my life to this day,

16 the Angel who has delivered me from all harm — may he bless these boys. May they be called by my name and the names of my fathers Abraham and Isaac. And may they grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth."
17 When Joseph saw that his father had placed his right hand on Ephraim's head, he was not happy. He took his father's hand to move it from Ephraim's head to Manasseh's head.
18 "Not so, my father!" Joseph said. "This one is the firstborn. Put your right hand on his head."

19 But his father refused. "I know, my son, I know," he said. "He too will become a people, and he too will become great. But his younger brother will be greater than he. His descendants will become a group of nations."
20 So that day Jacob blessed them and said: "By you Israel will say this blessing: 'May God make you like Ephraim and Manasseh.'" So he put Ephraim before Manasseh.
21 Then Israel said to Joseph, "Look, I am about to die. But God will be with you and bring you back to the land of your fathers.
22 And to you, one more share than your brothers, I give the piece of land that I took from the Amorites with my sword and my bow."

title: Genesis 49
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Then Jacob called for his sons. He said, "Gather around. I will tell you what will happen in the days to come.
2 Come together and listen, sons of Jacob. Listen to your father Israel.
3 Reuben, you are my firstborn. You are my might, the first sign of my strength. You excel in honor and in power.
4 But you are turbulent as the waters. You will no longer excel. For you went up onto your father's bed. You went onto my couch and defiled it.
5 Simeon and Levi are brothers. Their swords are weapons of violence.
6 May I never enter their council. May I never join their meeting. For they kill men in their anger. They cripple oxen for fun.
7 Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce! Cursed be their fury, for it is cruel! I will scatter them in Jacob. I will spread them out across Israel.
8 Judah, your brothers will praise you. Your hand will be on the neck of your enemies. Your father's sons will bow down to you.
9 Judah, you are a lion's cub. My son, you return from the prey. Like a lion he crouches and lies down. Like a lioness — who dares to wake him?
10 The scepter will not leave Judah. The ruler's staff will not leave from between his feet. It will stay until Shiloh comes. And the obedience of the nations will be his.
11 He ties his donkey to the vine. He ties his colt to the best branch. He washes his clothes in wine. He washes his robes in the blood of grapes.
12 His eyes are darker than wine. His teeth are whiter than milk.
13 Zebulun will live by the seashore. He will become a haven for ships. His border will reach to Sidon.
14 Issachar is a strong donkey. He lies down between two saddlebags.
15 He saw that his resting place was good. He saw that his land was pleasant. So he bent his shoulder to the burden. He submitted to forced labor.
16 Dan will provide justice for his people. He will be one of the tribes of Israel.
17 Dan will be a snake by the road. He will be a viper along the path. He bites the horse's heels. Then its rider tumbles backward.
18 I look for your deliverance, LORD.
19 Gad will be attacked by raiders. But he will attack them at their heels.
20 Asher's food will be rich. He will provide food fit for a king.
21 Naphtali is a doe set free. She bears beautiful fawns.
22 Joseph is a fruitful vine. He is a fruitful vine by a spring. His branches climb over the wall.
23 The archers attacked him with bitterness. They shot at him in hostility.
24 But his bow stayed steady. His strong arms stayed limber. This came from the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob. It came in the name of the Shepherd, the Rock of Israel.

25 It came from the God of your father, who helps you. It came from the Almighty, who blesses you. He blesses you with blessings of the skies above. He blesses you with blessings of the deep springs below. He blesses you with blessings of the breast and womb.

26 Your father's blessings are greater than the blessings of the ancient mountains. They are greater than the bounty of the everlasting hills. May they rest on the head of Joseph. May they rest on the brow of the prince among his brothers.
27 Benjamin is a hungry wolf. In the morning he devours the prey. In the evening he divides the plunder."
28 These are the tribes of Israel — twelve in all. This is what their father said to them. He blessed them. He gave each one a blessing that fit him.
29 Then Jacob gave them instructions. He said, "I am about to be gathered to my people. Bury me with my fathers. Bury me in the cave in the field of Ephron the Hittite.
30 The cave is in the field of Machpelah, near Mamre, in the land of Canaan. This is the field Abraham bought from Ephron the Hittite. He bought it as a burial place.
31 There Abraham and his wife Sarah are buried. There Isaac and his wife Rebekah are buried. And there I buried Leah.
32 The field and the cave in it were bought from the Hittites."
33 When Jacob had finished giving instructions to his sons, he pulled his feet up into the bed. He breathed his last. And he was gathered to his people.

title: Genesis 50
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Genesis
paragraphs: 1 Then Joseph threw himself on his father's face. He wept over him and kissed him.
2 Joseph told the doctors who served him to prepare his father's body. So they prepared the body of Israel.
3 This took the full forty days needed for preparing the body. And the Egyptians mourned for him seventy days.
4 When the days of mourning ended, Joseph spoke to Pharaoh's officials. He said, "If you are pleased with me, please tell Pharaoh this:

5 'My father made me promise with an oath. He said, "I am about to die. You must bury me in the grave that I dug for myself in the land of Canaan." Now let me go and bury my father, and then I will return.'"
6 Pharaoh replied, "Go up and bury your father, as he made you swear to do."
7 So Joseph went up to bury his father. All the servants of Pharaoh went with him. The important men of Pharaoh's house and all the important men of Egypt went too.
8 All of Joseph's household, and his brothers, and his father's household also went. Only their children and flocks and herds stayed in Goshen.
9 Chariots and horsemen also went up with him. It was a very large company.
10 They reached the threshing floor of Atad, beyond the Jordan. There they cried out loudly with great sadness. Joseph mourned for his father seven days.

11 The Canaanites of that land saw the mourning at the threshing floor of Atad. They said, "This is a sad ceremony of mourning by the Egyptians." So the place beyond the Jordan was called Abel-mizraim ("mourning of the Egyptians").
12 So Jacob's sons did what he had told them to do.
13 They carried him to the land of Canaan. They buried him in the cave at Machpelah, in the field near Mamre. Abraham had bought this field from Ephron the Hittite as a burial place.
14 After Joseph had buried his father, he returned to Egypt. His brothers and all who had gone with him to bury his father returned too.
15 When Joseph's brothers saw that their father was dead, they were afraid. They said, "What if Joseph holds a grudge against us? Then he will pay us back for all the wrong we did to him."
16 So they sent word to Joseph. They said, "Before he died, your father gave this command:

17 'This is what you are to say to Joseph: I beg you, please forgive the wrongs and sin of your brothers, for they did you wrong.' So now, Joseph, please forgive the wrongs of the servants of the God of your father." When their message came to him, Joseph wept.
18 His brothers also came to him. They bowed down before him and said, "We are your slaves!"
19 But Joseph replied, "Do not be afraid. Am I in the place of God?
20 You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good. He did this to bring about what is happening today — to save the lives of many people.
21 So do not be afraid. I will provide for you and your little ones." So Joseph comforted his brothers and spoke kindly to them.
22 Joseph and his father's household stayed in Egypt. Joseph lived to the age of 110.
23 He saw Ephraim's sons to the third generation. The sons of Machir son of Manasseh were also brought up on Joseph's knees.
24 Then Joseph said to his brothers, "I am about to die. But God will surely come to your aid. He will bring you up from this land to the land he promised on oath to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob."
25 And Joseph made the sons of Israel take an oath. He said, "God will surely come to your aid. Then you must carry my bones up from this place."
26 So Joseph died at the age of 110. They prepared his body, and placed it in a coffin in Egypt.
